sprint: Traveller Greeting Polish — Bigger Type, Gradual Fades, No More Chopped-Off Egg Pickup
task: Four concrete follow-ups on the shipped single-video Traveller pipeline: "make the text appear more polish. Increase the font. let it appear gradually and disappear gradually. right now its abrupts and running very quickly. there is jerk post last text and lumo seems to be abruptly flying. the sequence of him picking up the egg seems to be chopped off." (1) Font size raised a full step -- .gateway-greeting-bubble's clamp went from clamp(19px,2.8vw,29px) to clamp(24px,3.6vw,38px), the subtitle from clamp(13px,1.6vw,17px) to clamp(16px,2vw,21px). (2) The fade itself was redesigned, not just sped up or slowed down -- the old gateway-line-in keyframe spent only ~15%/18% of its own duration on the actual fade (a snap at 300ms/360ms out of 2000ms); the new keyframe (0%->26% fade in, hold, 74%->100% fade out) spends roughly a quarter of the line's own duration on each side, with a longer 18px drift instead of the old faint 10px nudge, and switched from ease-out to ease-in-out for a smoother glide. A dedicated GREETING_LINE_MS(2600ms) constant -- kept separate from the shared LINE_MS(2000) still used by Lumo's own arrival lines on the Returning Creator path -- gives a two-line title+subtitle more time to read. playLines() now also sets the animation's real duration inline (bubble.style.animationDuration=lineMs+'ms') for every caller, so the fade's actual speed always matches whatever hold time that specific call uses instead of a value hardcoded in CSS that could quietly drift out of sync with it. (3+4) The real root cause behind "lumo seems to be abruptly flying" and "the sequence of him picking up the egg seems to be chopped off": a frame-by-frame ffmpeg extraction (montage across 4.5s-8.2s) confirmed Segment 2 previously resumed at RESUME_AT_S=7.5, hard-skipping the video's own real footage between ~5s and ~7.3s -- exactly where Lumo crouches down, grabs the Story Egg back up, and leaps into flight -- so viewers never saw the pickup at all and Segment 2 always opened already mid-air. Frames 4.5s-6.0s all read as the same held "standing, wings out, Egg on the ground" pose, so resuming from the exact same timestamp Segment 1 paused at (RESUME_AT_S=PAUSE_AT_S=5, not a separate later value) costs nothing visually during the dialogue pause and removes the hard seek entirely -- playVideoSegmentTo() already snaps the video's own currentTime to PAUSE_AT_S when it pauses, so resuming from the identical value is a genuine no-op seek: the video simply un-pauses exactly where it left off and the crouch/grab/liftoff/flight now all play in full, continuously, for the first time. Verified via Playwright: font sizes measured directly (38px at 1280px viewport, up from the old 19-29px range); the fade's own opacity was sampled across the transition window and confirmed to ramp gradually (not snap 0->1); the animation's real computed duration matches GREETING_LINE_MS exactly; and -- the core fix -- the video's second play() call now fires with currentTime===5 (not ~7.5 as before), confirmed via a play()-call spy across the full sequence. The existing 15-assertion Traveller/Returning-Creator pipeline suite (re-timed for the new pacing and updated to assert the corrected resume point) plus the completion/tap-to-skip sanity checks and both goldenBuild.js suites all pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/gatewaySequence.js and css/style.css, with a matching cache-bust version bump on both stylesheet <link> tags in index.html.
